chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P.NO.S-1597 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For katcha peshi. 13.10.2017 Mr. Bhagwandas Bheel, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Ghulam Abbas Sangi, Assistant A.G a/w Inspector Ali Nawaz SHO P.S Mari Jalbani. Respondents No.6 and 8 present in person. = With reference to the notice of this case, learned A.A.G files parawise comments on behalf of respondents No.3, 4 and 5, which are taken on record and copies whereof have been supplied to the learned Counsel for the petitioner. Whereas, Mr. Imam Bux Khaskheli, Advocate files power on behalf of the respondents No.7 & 9, which is also taken on record. On perusal of the comments, it appears that there are some cases against the petitioner arising out of certain FIRs are pending adjudication before the concerned Courts, whereas, the official respondent No.3 in his statement has stated that the petitioner has never approached him against the alleged highhandedness of private respondents and for any protection, however, it has been assured that the protection will be provided to the petitioner in the event he approaches the official respondents. Learned Counsel for the private respondents stated that some FIRs have been lodged against the petitioner and the petitioner is not harassed by the respondents, however, he undertakes that the respondents shall restrict themselves from causing any harassment to the petitioner in future. Record further reveals that the parties have lodged cases against each other, which are pending adjudication before the competent Court of Law.

Learned Counsel for the private respondents stated that some FIRs have been lodged against the petitioner and the petitioner is not harassed by the respondents, however, he undertakes that the respondents shall restrict themselves from causing any harassment to the petitioner in future. Record further reveals that the parties have lodged cases against each other, which are pending adjudication before the competent Court of Law. In the above facts and circumstances, the parties are directed not to take law in their hands and if they have any grievance against each other, they may approach the concerned Court for redressal of their grievance, whereas, the official respondents are directed to provide protection to the parties in the event they approach them but strictly in accordance with law. The official respondents are further directed to discharge their functions and duties strictly in accordance with law in a fair and reasonable manner without patronizing any group or individuals. Petition stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE Shahid
